decisions.description: All configurable values must come from config.py or environment variables. Hardcoded values anywhere else are a merge blocker without exception.
decisions.rationale: Had a production incident where timeout was hardcoded to 30s in one service and 60s in another, causing inconsistent behavior. Config.py is the single source of truth.

decisions.description: Maximum retry attempts is 3. This value must come from Config.RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS. Any PR setting a different value or hardcoding attempts is a merge blocker.
decisions.rationale: Agreed after load testing showed 5 attempts caused thundering herd during outages. 3 with exponential backoff is the sweet spot for our SLAs.

decisions.description: Do not use singleton pattern for service classes. Use dependency injection instead.
decisions.rationale: Singletons cause test isolation issues in our parallel pytest suite. DI allows proper mocking.

decisions.description: All service exceptions must be wrapped in ServiceException (or its subclasses) with error_code and user_message fields. Raw exceptions (ValueError, RuntimeError, etc.) must never propagate to callers.
decisions.rationale: API consumers need consistent error shapes. Raw exceptions were leaking implementation details to clients.

decisions.description: The circuit breaker in PaymentProcessor must never be bypassed or removed. Any PR that removes the can_proceed() check or adds a flag to skip it is a merge blocker.
decisions.rationale: Had a cascading failure when the circuit breaker was commented out during debugging and accidentally merged. Cost us 45 minutes of downtime.

decisions.description: Idempotency cache TTL must come from Config.IDEMPOTENCY_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS. In-memory dict is acceptable for current scale. Redis migration planned for Q3.
decisions.rationale: Current load is <100 req/s. In-memory is fine. But TTL must be configurable so we can tune without deploys.
